rationale: This supervisor structure prioritizes safe, efficient exploration by preferring forward movement in clear conditions, using single-action commit states to avoid oscillations, and employing recovery/scan actions when obstacles are detected. Helper states (e.g., forward_commit, rotate_commit_cw, rotate_commit_ccw, scan_full, recover_back) ensure that each controllable action is atomic and transitions back to a perception/decision state. The design prevents infinite loops, avoids enabling multiple controllables per state, and ensures all uncontrollable events are handled deterministically. Recovery from obstacles always passes through a commit or scan state before resuming exploration.
strategy: Prefer forward movement in clear conditions to maximize coverage.
When an obstacle is detected in front, escape by moving backward or performing a full scan.
If an obstacle is detected on the left, prefer rotating clockwise to avoid it.
If an obstacle is detected on the right, prefer rotating counterclockwise to avoid it.
All controllable actions are performed in atomic commit states, ensuring no oscillations or repeated actions.
After recovery (move_backward), perform a full scan to reorient before resuming exploration.
All uncontrollable events are handled deterministically in every state.
No infinite controllable loops; every commit state transitions back to a perception/decision state.
Helper states ensure one-shot actions and clear separation between action and sensing phases.
